title: neanotis ingrata
intro: Neanotis ingrata, also known as the "Indian squill," is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Gentianaceae. It has been traditionally used in various parts of the world, particularly in India and China, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Neanotis ingrata include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use as a natural remedy for fever and sore throats. Neanotis ingrata can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, decoctions, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
